steps.title: Spot the past time reference
steps.content: The phrase “in 1868” establishes a specific point in the past, signaling that the verb must express a completed historical action.

steps.title: Apply the finite verb rule
steps.content:
A main clause requires a finite verb in simple past tense for a specific past event. B (began) is the only finite, past-tense form here.

The other choices are non-finite forms (infinitive, participles) that cannot function as main verbs.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
